question: How does the chargeback report attribute spend to domains?
expectedAnswer: It joins real Azure Cost Management actual spend to governance domains via the loom-domain resource tag plus the cost-attribution ledger of per-execution costs — the Azure-native 1:1 of the Fabric Chargeback app, with a stacked bar chart, per-workspace drill-down, and CSV export.

question: Can I drill chargeback down to individual users?
expectedAnswer: Yes — expand any domain or workspace to see the per-user execution costs recorded at each Spark, Databricks, ADX, and AOAI submit, from the append-only cost-attribution ledger (TTL 90d).

question: What RBAC does the chargeback report need?
expectedAnswer: The Console UAMI needs Cost Management Reader on the billing scope. If the grant is absent the page shows the exact role and scope to assign, and the cost-attribution ledger still renders its per-execution view.

question: What is a Loom Capacity Unit (LCU)?
expectedAnswer: The LCU is Loom's normalization of real Azure Monitor utilization across every engine it orchestrates (ACA, Spark, Synapse, ADX, Cosmos) to one capacity scale, with Cost Management spend joined alongside — the Azure-native 1:1 of the Fabric Capacity Metrics app.

question: What does the throttle/surge gauge on Usage & chargeback reflect?
expectedAnswer: The surge-protection state from the capacity-guardrails policy — utilization vs the capacity-level rejection threshold — so an admin can see when admission control is shaping load.

question: Where does the $/mo column on /admin/capacity come from?
expectedAnswer: A real Microsoft.CostManagement/query POST (ActualCost with a ResourceId dimension filter) per resource, formatted currency-aware from the query's Currency column, with a roll-up footer over loaded rows.

question: What backs the per-row utilization sparkline on the capacity page?
expectedAnswer: Azure Monitor platform metrics via the microsoft.insights/metrics REST — the headline metric per resource type, with a detail drawer of all platform metric charts.

question: What shows in the cost cell when Cost Management access is missing?
expectedAnswer: An honest 'No access' badge with a tooltip — never a fake number. Similarly a type with no platform metric shows a dash and a MessageBar in the detail pane.

question: Which roles does the capacity cost/utilization page require on the Console UAMI?
expectedAnswer: Cost Management Reader (via cost-management-reader-rbac.bicep) and Monitoring Reader (via monitoring-reader-rbac.bicep), both at subscription scope.

question: What does a Loom deployment cost when idle, and how do I cut active cost?
expectedAnswer: The idle floor is about $1-2K/month (APIM, Container Apps, AI Search, Key Vault, Front Door, Purview bill continuously); active F8 use runs ~$3-5K/month. Pause Databricks and ADX from Admin > Capacity to cut active DBU/cluster cost.

question: Does the cost column work in Gov clouds?
expectedAnswer: Yes — GCC uses the same ARM host, GCC-High/IL5 go through management.usgovcloudapi.net for EA/PAYG offers (CSP offers show an honest gate), and DoD uses its own management endpoint. Utilization metrics work in every cloud.

question: How is Fabric capacity CU% shown, and what about Gov?
expectedAnswer: cu_percentage is read as an Azure Monitor platform metric on the Microsoft.Fabric/capacities ARM type — never via a Fabric REST call. In Gov clouds, where no Fabric capacity exists, the cell shows an honest dash instead of a fabricated value.
